customer_anxiety_profile: Low-affect, cooperative but not engaged. Larry's skepticism is passive — he asks clarifying questions rather than objecting, and he resolves his own concerns before the rep addresses them. He is not hostile, but he has no emotional investment in the appointment. The closer should not assume warmth.
unresolved_concerns: Larry flagged prior Ion text contact ('I've already gotten you texts. I've responded to it.') — the source of that prior outreach was not clarified. The closer should be prepared for Larry to reference this again.

Larry's question 'What does being on the schedule mean?' was answered at the surface level but the underlying legitimacy concern was not resolved. Larry may arrive at the appointment without a clear picture of what he agreed to.
Larry's wife is on the title and was not included in the appointment. She is an unaddressed decision-maker.
sensitive_topics_raised: Prior Ion contact / text history — Larry mentioned receiving texts from Ion before this call and having responded. Identity or contact-source confusion is possible.
exact_promises_rep_made: Bucky will text a reminder after the call.
The appointment is with a specialist who will provide an estimate and answer questions.
No upfront cost — only a lower monthly solar bill replaces the current utility bill.
If Ion cannot save Larry money in the first year, they are not allowed to offer anything.
closer_should_know: Larry's wife is on the title and was not included in the appointment — confirm at the top of the call whether she is available, and if not, treat spouse inclusion as the first agenda item before proceeding to the design. The 'no savings, no offer' promise Bucky made at 03:17 must be honored or explicitly addressed; Larry may hold the closer to it.
